title: Git→FUSE propagation for operator-direct and scheduled-agent commits: extend workspace-sync to invoke build.js sync-workspace after each push cycle, closing the recurring FUSE-stale-prompts failure class observed in FND-01 (2026-05-25) and FND-03 (2026-05-26).
author: tinker
source_directive: DIRECTIVE-20260526-001 (operator-bumped to HIGH 2026-05-26T20:30Z after three consecutive tinker runs flagged the same gap).
executive_summary: The hourly workspace-sync agent is FUSE→git only. Any commit that lands on origin/main from a path OTHER than 'node build.js publish' (operator-direct via clone, GitHub Git Data API push, scheduled-agent push from a clone, decider self-apply that skips publish) leaves FUSE permanently stale relative to git for the affected files. The defect is most visible on prompt files: monitor/prompts/tinker.md (FUSE 314 lines vs git 400), monitor/prompts/decider.md (FUSE 833 vs git 1037), monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md (FUSE 624 vs git 647) measured at the time of this PROP. The agents read prompts from FUSE, so they execute pre-update logic. The fix mechanism ALREADY EXISTS in build.js (function syncToWorkspace, target 'sync-workspace', PROP-010) — it iterates OWNERSHIP entries with category='git' plus the monitor/prompts/*.md dynamic rule and copies clone→workspace. The gap is that nothing INVOKES sync-workspace except 'build.js publish'. This PROP extends workspace-sync.md to invoke 'node build.js sync-workspace' after the FUSE→git push completes, in two phases (Phase 1 read-only divergence detector for 1 week of observation; Phase 2 auto-sync). Non-fast-forward (force-pushed reset) aborts via a new sentinel-file gate, mirroring the PROP-051 A4 gate pattern.

observed_incidents_72h.finding: FND-01 — 7 stale FUSE files after 4 operator-direct commits in 10h. Tinker manually backfilled all 7 from raw.githubusercontent.com. Cost: ~5 min Opus.
Same defect restated across two consecutive runs. No git→FUSE pull path exists for operator-direct commits.

FND-03 — FUSE tinker.md is 314 lines (pre-PROP-060) vs git 400 lines. Caused tinker's pre-flight directive-discovery to phantom-pickup DIRECTIVE-20260525-003 as 'pending' even though git has it 'completed' since 2026-05-25T17:30Z.
FND-01-RECUR — phantom directive pickup recurred a SECOND time. ~$2 Opus burn. FUSE tinker.md still pre-PROP-060.
Confirmed via direct md5 comparison: tinker.md, decider.md, structure-integrity.md ALL stale in FUSE relative to git. decider.md gap is 204 lines (FUSE 833 vs git 1037) — substantial drift.

problem.root_cause: workspace-sync is direction-asymmetric. It pushes FUSE→git (with universal-pusher rescue, anti-reversion guard, and PROP-051 A1–A4 safety gates) but does NOT pull git→FUSE. The only existing git→FUSE path is the syncToWorkspace() function inside build.js, which only runs when 'node build.js publish' is invoked (decider self-apply path). Three publish-bypassing write paths exist and accumulate FUSE-stale state: (1) Operator-direct: cd /tmp/edit-clone && git push (frequent — used for prompt edits, CLAUDE.md edits, ad-hoc commits); (2) GitHub Git Data API push (PROP-050) — bypasses build.js entirely; (3) Scheduled-agent push from a clone WITHOUT calling build.js publish (poller, analyst-baby, sloppytoppy-score, sloppytoppy-rewrite, workspace-sync, prune-integrity all push via their own clones for their own data files). The asymmetry compounds: the operator's habit is to use option-1 because /tmp/edit-clone + git push is faster than the publish cycle. Every such commit makes FUSE staler.
problem.defect_class: Write-side discipline without read-side enforcement. Same shape as the writer-no-consumer defects PROP-060 just closed (assigned-to-tinker fields written by analyst/decider/integrity with no tinker reader) and the still-open action-typed HNOTE handler (narrow consumer for narrative HNOTEs). The general pattern: a write path exists, but the read path is incidental or absent. workspace-sync is the obvious reader-side actor for git→FUSE because it already owns FUSE↔git boundary work and runs hourly.

alternatives_considered.name: Workspace-sync extension (this PROP's recommendation)
alternatives_considered.summary: After each FUSE→git push cycle, workspace-sync (a) does 'git fetch origin main && git merge --ff-only', (b) invokes 'node build.js sync-workspace' which is the existing PROP-010 mechanism, (c) if --ff-only fails (force-pushed reset), aborts with a sentinel mirroring the PROP-051 A4 delete-gate pattern. Phased: Phase 1 read-only divergence detector for 1 week, Phase 2 auto-sync.
pros: Mechanism already exists (build.js syncToWorkspace function + 'sync-workspace' target, PROP-010). Zero new file-copy logic. The only addition is a workspace-sync.md invocation block.
Single source of truth for what gets synced: build.js OWNERSHIP table. No drift risk between workspace-sync's deny-list and build.js's allow-list.
Hourly cadence — much faster than the current 'wait for next decider publish' implicit cadence, which is now ~6/day in BAU but can drop to once-per-day in burndown mode.

Composable with PROP-051 safety gates — git→FUSE is purely additive (copyFileSync, no deletes), so the disaster vector (mass-delete) is structurally impossible. The non-FF reset abort handles the only failure mode worth aborting on.
Mirrors the universal-pusher addition (2026-04-26): same agent, same hourly cycle, additional direction. Conceptually clean.
cons: Workspace-sync is Haiku and was the agent that hit the 2026-05-21 mass-delete disaster. Expanding its scope adds risk surface even though the new direction is structurally safer than the deletion-capable FUSE→git direction.

Adds one explicit exception to the existing prompt rule 'Do NOT run node build.js publish, node build.js pdf, or node test.js' — the new exception is 'sync-workspace target only, never publish'. The prompt must clearly enumerate the allowed target.

Phase 2 makes FUSE downstream of origin/main on hourly cadence. If a bad commit lands on main, FUSE inherits within ≤1h. Mitigation: workspace-sync's existing JSON-validity gate operates on the FUSE→git direction (catches FUSE-bad-JSON before it commits); the inverse direction is git→FUSE where git is already-committed and assumed-valid.

Force-pushed reset (e.g. 2026-05-21 disaster recovery) needs an abort path. Without it, a reset that mass-deletes git would propagate to FUSE via the same mechanism. The proposed non-FF abort gate handles this — but it is new logic the operator must verify.

alternatives_considered.name: Discipline rule: operator-direct commits dual-write FUSE
alternatives_considered.summary: Add to CLAUDE.md's File Ownership Rules: 'When operator-direct-committing to a git-owned file, also cp the file from the clone to FUSE before pushing.' Plus an integrity check (no enforcement).
pros: Zero new code. Zero new prompt edits beyond CLAUDE.md.
cons: Pure discipline. The human-notes dual-write rule (already in CLAUDE.md, same shape) has been violated multiple times this month, including by the operator and by multiple scheduled agents.
Doesn't cover scheduled-agent pushes from clones (poller, analyst-baby, sloppytoppy-*, etc.) — they would all need the same touch.
Doesn't cover PROP-050 Git Data API pushes (operator escape hatch when git push 403s).
Same defect class as the directive's source finding (writer/consumer asymmetry) — adding more writer-side discipline without any consumer is exactly the antipattern we're fixing.

alternatives_considered.name: Per-agent pre-flight divergence gate
alternatives_considered.summary: Each agent that reads from FUSE adds a pre-flight check: for each file it depends on (its own prompt, the data files it consumes), it fetches the corresponding raw.githubusercontent.com version and either matches-or-aborts. Tinker already does some of this; generalize across all agents.
pros: Localized defense at point of read. Each agent owns its own check.
Detects in addition to fixing — if the gate fires, we learn about staleness even if Phase 2 of option_a doesn't ship.
cons: Distributed change: every agent prompt needs the touch. 11 agents currently active; not all have HTTP fetch capability (Haiku poller, workspace-sync) without prompt edits.
Doesn't FIX staleness — just detects it. Agents would fail-or-warn but the prompt would still be stale next run.
Adds 2-5s of latency per agent run (the GitHub raw fetch) and a small but nonzero risk of pre-flight false positives (GitHub raw cache TTL is ~5 min; fresh commits aren't immediately visible).
Doesn't address the operator's pain point: cowork sessions repeatedly noticing FUSE-vs-git divergence and needing to manually reconcile.

alternatives_considered.name: Hybrid: option_a for prompts + scripts, option_c for data files
alternatives_considered.summary: workspace-sync invokes sync-workspace for monitor/prompts/*.md + build-scripts/* + build.js + test.js + CLAUDE.md only. Data files (wins.json, sections.json, predictions.json, decision state) stay on the current 'build.js publish syncs them when decider self-applies' path.
pros: Narrow blast radius. The observed pain is entirely prompts; this targets that exactly.
Data files are already well-handled by build.js publish + the universal-pusher rescue.
cons: Two parallel sync paths to maintain. Operator must remember which files use which path.
build.js sync-workspace already does both in one call. Splitting it requires either a new build.js target or a workspace-sync-side filter loop. Either way, more code.

The data file staleness has been ZERO recently because the decider publishes ~6×/day. But it could regress (e.g. burndown mode lowers decider cadence). Belt-and-suspenders is cheaper than re-debugging that regression later.

alternatives_considered.name: Standalone scheduled task: dome-fuse-pull every 30 min
alternatives_considered.summary: New scheduled task (Haiku, narrow prompt) whose only job is 'git fetch + sync-workspace'. workspace-sync stays unchanged.
pros: Cleanest separation of concerns. workspace-sync remains FUSE→git only.
cons: Adds a 12th scheduled task to a pipeline the operator is already pruning (sloppytoppy-* DISABLED, dome-tinker burndown mode discussions).
Doubles the disk-pressure surface — two ephemeral clones instead of one, both subject to disk-fill leaks.
Coordination latency: if dome-fuse-pull runs 5 min after a workspace-sync push, agents see a 5-min window of FUSE-stale state. Co-located in workspace-sync, the window is 0.
More expensive in tokens for the same work.

recommendation_rationale: Option (a) is the smallest change that uses the existing build.js sync-workspace mechanism. The implementation is one block of bash in workspace-sync.md, gated by a non-FF abort and split into Phase 1 (detector) and Phase 2 (action) so the operator gets a week of observation before any FUSE writes happen. Option (b) is rejected by the directive's own tradeoff analysis (discipline without enforcement). Option (c) is useful as belt-and-suspenders but doesn't FIX the gap. Option (d) is option (a) with an unnecessary narrow-scope filter — the build.js OWNERSHIP table already declares what's git-owned; respecting that contract is cheaper than re-litigating which subset to sync. Option (e) is more expensive for the same outcome.

counter_considerations.concern: Workspace-sync was the agent that caused the 2026-05-21 mass-delete disaster. Expanding its scope adds risk to the agent we know can fail catastrophically.
counter_considerations.resolution: The 2026-05-21 failure was on the FUSE→git direction with a Haiku LLM improvising a no-checkout clone fallback. The git→FUSE direction is structurally different: it only does copyFileSync from a checked-out clone to FUSE. It cannot delete FUSE files (build.js syncToWorkspace has no delete logic). It cannot delete git files (it doesn't touch git after the fetch+merge). The remaining risk vector is 'clobber a FUSE file that has unpushed FUSE-only content' — but workspace-sync's own FUSE→git push ran FIRST in the cycle, so any FUSE-newer content has already been pushed to git. Anything left in FUSE that disagrees with origin/main after the push is, by construction, stale FUSE content.

counter_considerations.concern: What if FUSE has unstaged local changes when sync-workspace runs?
counter_considerations.resolution: FUSE doesn't have 'staged' vs 'unstaged' state — every write to FUSE is an agent write, and the next workspace-sync FUSE→git push moves it to git. The race window is: agent A writes FUSE-X at T, agent B commits FUSE-X to git at T+2min via clone, workspace-sync runs at T+5min. The FUSE→git step picks up A's FUSE-X (via universal-pusher rescue with anti-reversion guard, which catches this exact pattern — see workspace-sync.md PROP-045). Then the git→FUSE step picks up the latest origin/main and copies FUSE-X back. If A's content is the same as B's (typical for hourly cadence races), this is a no-op. If they differ, FUSE-X ends up with origin/main's content — which is the correct outcome because the next agent that reads FUSE-X should see git's canonical state.

counter_considerations.concern: What if origin/main has been force-pushed (2026-05-21 disaster recovery, or any future operator-initiated rewrite)?
counter_considerations.resolution: Non-FF abort gate. After 'git fetch origin main', workspace-sync checks if the local HEAD is an ancestor of origin/main (git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/main). If NOT (which means origin has been rewritten), workspace-sync writes monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-<ts>.json with {reason, local_sha, remote_sha, fetch_log} and exits the git→FUSE step (the FUSE→git push has already succeeded, so no data loss). Operator inspects the sentinel, decides whether to force-reset the clone or take other action. This mirrors the PROP-051 A1–A4 fail-closed gate pattern. Importantly: in a force-push-recovery scenario like 2026-05-21, FUSE may contain the pre-disaster CORRECT state (because the disaster was the git delete, not a FUSE delete) — in which case we EXPLICITLY do not want to overwrite FUSE with the post-disaster reset. The abort gate preserves FUSE in this scenario, which is the right call.

counter_considerations.concern: What if the file is binary or huge (e.g. docs/assets/*.svg, future docs/assets/*.png)?
counter_considerations.resolution: fs.copyFileSync handles arbitrary sizes and binary content. The OWNERSHIP table is curated and explicit; no surprise files. Phase 1's read-only divergence detector outputs the would-copy file list so any unexpected file appears in the sentinel before Phase 2 ships.

counter_considerations.concern: What if 'node build.js sync-workspace' fails (e.g. node missing, build.js corrupt, OWNERSHIP table parse error)?
counter_considerations.resolution: workspace-sync catches the non-zero exit code, logs to its run report under agent_notes 'fallback: sync-workspace exit code N', and continues. The FUSE→git push has already succeeded. Tinker's existing soft-complaints grep (which scans workspace-sync-runs/*.json for 'fallback', 'warning', etc.) surfaces it on the next tinker run.

counter_considerations.concern: Tinker's phantom-pickup of completed directives is the canary that drove this PROP. If Phase 2 ships and the gap closes, tinker loses that detection mechanism — how do we know the fix is still working in 6 months?
counter_considerations.resolution: Two ways: (1) the new monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json sentinel emitted by Phase 2 records each git→FUSE copy event with file count + run timestamp. Tinker's existing soft-complaints grep can scan these for prolonged absence (>24h with no sync-workspace sentinel = mechanism broken). (2) The Phase 1 divergence detector remains in place after Phase 2 ships — if Phase 2 is working, the detector should report ZERO divergence post-sync. Any non-zero post-sync divergence is a regression.

counter_considerations.concern: Doesn't this just push the problem to the next layer? What if workspace-sync itself is broken (disk pressure, PAT 403, etc.) — FUSE diverges again.
counter_considerations.resolution: True. But the failure mode is now self-healing rather than progressive: when workspace-sync is healthy again, it pulls everything up to date in the next cycle. Today, the failure mode is progressive — every operator-direct commit makes FUSE staler, and there's no automatic catch-up. Even a 24h workspace-sync outage with PROP-061 leaves at most a 1h tail of staleness after recovery. Today's 24h workspace-sync outage leaves a permanent diff until tinker manually backfills.

phase_1_readonly_detector.scope: After successful FUSE→git push, workspace-sync does 'git fetch origin main' (no merge), computes 'git diff --name-only HEAD origin/main', and if non-empty writes monitor/integrity/git-to-fuse-divergence-<ts>.json with {timestamp, local_sha, remote_sha, divergent_files: [path...], divergent_count, would_abort_for_non_ff: boolean}. No file copies. No FUSE writes.
phase_1_readonly_detector.duration: 1 week of observation. Operator reviews the divergence reports daily to confirm the file list matches expectations (prompts, scripts, CLAUDE.md, data files when an operator-direct commit lands).
phase_1_readonly_detector.exit_criteria: Operator approves Phase 2 promotion. Tinker writes a 7-day summary report from monitor/integrity/git-to-fuse-divergence-*.json: total divergence events, mean files-per-event, max files-per-event, any non-FF events.
phase_1_readonly_detector.rollback: Trivially reversible by removing the new block from workspace-sync.md. No persistent state changed.
phase_1_readonly_detector.implementation_pseudocode:
After Step 4 (commit + push) in workspace-sync.md:

git fetch origin main 2>&1 | tail -3
LOCAL_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
REMOTE_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
if [ "$LOCAL_SHA" != "$REMOTE_SHA" ]; then
  if git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/main; then WOULD_FF=true; else WOULD_FF=false; fi
  DIVERGENT_FILES=$(git diff --name-only HEAD origin/main | sort -u)
  DIVERGENT_COUNT=$(echo "$DIVERGENT_FILES" | grep -c .)
  TS=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ)
  OUT="${WORKSPACE}/monitor/integrity/git-to-fuse-divergence-${TS}.json"
  WOULD_FF="$WOULD_FF" LOCAL_SHA="$LOCAL_SHA" REMOTE_SHA="$REMOTE_SHA" \
  DIVERGENT_FILES="$DIVERGENT_FILES" DIVERGENT_COUNT="$DIVERGENT_COUNT" \
  TS="$TS" OUT="$OUT" node -e '<emit JSON>'
fi
# DO NOT merge. DO NOT call sync-workspace. Phase 1 is read-only.

phase_2_auto_sync.scope: Same fetch as Phase 1, plus: if would_ff=true, run 'git merge --ff-only origin/main' then 'node build.js sync-workspace'. If would_ff=false, write monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-<ts>.json (sentinel) and skip the merge+sync without erroring (FUSE→git push has already succeeded).
phase_2_auto_sync.trigger_for_promotion: Phase 1 has run 7 days clean (no non-FF aborts other than known operator-driven resets). Divergence-event file list matches expectations. Operator explicitly approves Phase 2 via HNOTE or directive.
phase_2_auto_sync.rollback: Remove the merge + sync-workspace lines from workspace-sync.md; Phase 1 detector remains. No persistent state changed (sync-workspace is idempotent — re-running it produces no different result).
phase_2_auto_sync.implementation_pseudocode:
After Phase 1's divergence detection block, if DIVERGENT_COUNT > 0:

if [ "$WOULD_FF" = "true" ]; then
  git merge --ff-only origin/main 2>&1 | tail -3 || { echo skip; exit_ok; }
  node build.js sync-workspace 2>&1 | tail -10
  SYNC_EXIT=$?
  # Emit run sentinel monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-${TS}.json with copied_paths and exit_code
else
  # Non-FF: origin rewritten. Emit non-ff abort sentinel and skip.
fi

phase_3_deferred.scope: Optional belt-and-suspenders: pre-flight divergence check in each agent (option_c equivalent). Only consider if Phase 2 misses real-world drift cases. Not in scope of this PROP.
phase_3_deferred.trigger_for_consideration: Phase 2 observed drift event where workspace-sync was healthy but an agent still read stale FUSE state for a file it depends on.

implementation_steps_phase_1.what: Edit monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md to add a new 'Step 4c: Phase 1 git→FUSE divergence detector (PROP-061, read-only)' block between the existing Step 4b (run report) and Step 5 (cleanup).
implementation_steps_phase_1.guard: The block runs ONLY if the prior 'git push origin main' succeeded (check exit code). On push failure, skip the detector — we don't have a fresh clone state to compare from.

implementation_steps_phase_1.what: Add a one-paragraph entry to workspace-sync.md's 'Rules' section: '- Phase 1 git→FUSE detector (PROP-061, read-only): After the FUSE→git push, this agent does git fetch origin main and writes a divergence report to monitor/integrity/git-to-fuse-divergence-<ts>.json if local HEAD differs from origin/main. NO FUSE writes in Phase 1. Phase 2 (auto-sync) ships after operator approval of 7 days of Phase 1 logs.'
Add monitor/integrity/git-to-fuse-divergence-*.json to the prune-integrity.js retention windows (suggest 14d retention, matching the verify-pending-run window).

No build.js changes in Phase 1. No CLAUDE.md changes in Phase 1 (the file ownership table still describes the correct ownership; the new sentinel files are append-only per-run reports under monitor/integrity/, already classified).

implementation_steps_phase_2.what: Extend the workspace-sync.md Step 4c block: after the divergence detector logs the report, if DIVERGENT_COUNT > 0 AND WOULD_FF=true, run 'git merge --ff-only origin/main' then 'node build.js sync-workspace'. Emit monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json sentinel.
Add non-FF abort path: if WOULD_FF=false, emit monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-<ts>.json sentinel and skip the merge+sync.

Update the 'CRITICAL: Degraded-mode prohibitions' section to ALLOW 'node build.js sync-workspace' as an explicit exception to the 'Do NOT run node build.js publish/pdf/test.js' rule. The exception is narrow: sync-workspace ONLY, never publish, pdf, or other targets.

Add monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json and monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-*.json to prune-integrity.js retention windows (suggest 14d for runs, 90d for non-FF aborts — the latter are rare and operator-significant).

Update CLAUDE.md's 'FUSE Staleness Warning' section to add: 'As of PROP-061 Phase 2 (date), workspace-sync runs a git→FUSE auto-sync after each FUSE→git push using node build.js sync-workspace. Operator-direct commits, GitHub Git Data API pushes, and scheduled-agent-clone pushes now propagate to FUSE within ≤1h. Force-pushed resets abort the sync via the non-FF gate; operator inspects monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-*.json sentinels.'

Update the Universal-pusher mode paragraph (which describes the FUSE→git direction) to cross-reference the new git→FUSE direction added by this PROP: 'workspace-sync is now bidirectional: universal-pusher (2026-04-26) for FUSE→git rescue + PROP-061 (this date) for git→FUSE propagation.'

file_ownership_impact.build_js_OWNERSHIP_changes: None. The mechanism uses the existing OWNERSHIP table as-is; no new entries.
file_ownership_impact.workspace_sync_md_NEVER_PUSH_changes: None. The new sentinel files live under monitor/integrity/ which is append_only — they don't round-trip via workspace-sync (workspace-sync writes them in the clone and pushes them as part of its own commit).

new_classified_files.purpose: Phase 2 fail-closed gate sentinel for force-pushed resets.

idempotency_contract.phase_1: Re-running the divergence detector with no new commits produces an empty divergent_files list — written either as a no-op (skip the file write if DIVERGENT_COUNT=0) or as a zero-count report (decide at implementation time; prefer skip for noise reduction). Either way, no FUSE state changes.
idempotency_contract.phase_2: Re-running sync-workspace on an already-synced FUSE is structurally idempotent. build.js syncToWorkspace uses fs.copyFileSync which overwrites if dst exists; if dst content matches src, the operation is logically a no-op (the byte content doesn't change, only mtime). The append_only walker skips files that already exist in FUSE. Multiple runs in the same hour produce identical FUSE state.
idempotency_contract.atomicity: fs.copyFileSync is atomic at the OS level (rename pattern). No partial-write FUSE state. The Phase 2 sentinel write is also atomic (Node fs.writeFileSync uses open+write+close).

edge_cases.case: FUSE has unstaged local changes when sync-workspace runs
edge_cases.behavior: Step 1-3 of workspace-sync ran FIRST and pushed any FUSE-newer content to git via universal-pusher (with mtime guard and anti-reversion guard). Any FUSE state remaining at Phase 2's sync-workspace step is, by construction, stale relative to git. copyFileSync overwrites — correct behavior.

git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/main returns false. WOULD_FF=false. Phase 1: divergence report records the non-FF state; tinker sees it. Phase 2: non-FF abort path writes the sentinel and SKIPS the merge + sync-workspace; FUSE is preserved. Operator inspects the sentinel and decides whether to force-reset the local clone for the next cycle.

edge_cases.case: File added in git but not in FUSE (typical: new agent prompt or new build script)
edge_cases.behavior: build.js syncToWorkspace's copyIfExists creates FUSE-side dirs as needed (fs.mkdirSync with recursive:true) and copies the file. FUSE gets the new file.

edge_cases.case: File deleted in git (extremely rare for OWNERSHIP entries)
edge_cases.behavior: build.js syncToWorkspace does NOT delete FUSE files — its loop only copies src→dst if src exists. FUSE retains the deleted file. This is a minor known limitation; in practice OWNERSHIP entries are never deleted (only re-classified). If a file IS removed from OWNERSHIP, FUSE retains the orphan until manual cleanup. Acceptable tradeoff — orphans are inert.

edge_cases.case: Binary or huge file (e.g. future docs/assets/*.png)
edge_cases.behavior: fs.copyFileSync handles arbitrary file sizes and binary content efficiently. No special handling needed.

edge_cases.case: build.js sync-workspace crashes (node missing, build.js corrupt, OWNERSHIP parse error)
edge_cases.behavior: workspace-sync captures the non-zero exit code, logs to agent_notes 'fallback: sync-workspace exit code N', and continues. The FUSE→git push has already succeeded. Tinker's existing soft-complaints grep scans run reports for 'fallback' on the next tinker run and surfaces the issue.

edge_cases.case: Disk pressure prevents git fetch (no space for new objects)
edge_cases.behavior: git fetch fails. Phase 1: skip the detector for this cycle, log to agent_notes. Phase 2: ditto, no sync-workspace this cycle. Next cycle retries. Same model as the existing PROP-051 A2 disk-pressure gate.

edge_cases.case: Concurrent push race: between our fetch and our merge --ff-only, another commit lands on origin/main
edge_cases.behavior: git merge --ff-only fails with 'Not possible to fast-forward'. We log and skip sync-workspace this cycle. The leftover content gets picked up next cycle. No FUSE writes happen. (This race window is ~2 seconds; rare but possible.)

edge_cases.case: Agent reads FUSE between Phase 2 merge and sync-workspace step
edge_cases.behavior: FUSE is in pre-sync state; agent sees old content. Window is ~5 seconds. Same characteristic as the existing FUSE staleness — Phase 2 narrows but doesn't eliminate the window. Phase 3 (option_c per-agent pre-flight checks) would close this, but is out of scope.

edge_cases.case: PROP-061 itself ships to git and FUSE workspace-sync.md is stale (chicken-and-egg)
edge_cases.behavior: Operator-side or decider-side commit applies PROP-061 to monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md in git. FUSE workspace-sync.md remains stale until the NEXT workspace-sync run. That run uses the OLD prompt (no PROP-061 logic). The run after (assuming the operator manually cp'd the new workspace-sync.md to FUSE OR ran 'node build.js sync-workspace' once OR pushed via 'node build.js publish' which auto-syncs) uses the NEW prompt. So PROP-061 needs ONE manual catalyst event (one `node build.js sync-workspace` invocation, or one `cp` from clone to FUSE) to bootstrap. After that, it self-sustains.

verification_pattern.phase_1: After implementation, verify by: (1) operator-direct edit a prompt file: cd /tmp/edit-clone && git pull && echo 'test' >> monitor/prompts/tinker.md && git commit -am 'test' && git push. (2) Wait for next hourly workspace-sync run (or trigger manually). (3) Read monitor/integrity/ — expect a git-to-fuse-divergence-<ts>.json file listing monitor/prompts/tinker.md. (4) FUSE state: monitor/prompts/tinker.md MUST still be unchanged (Phase 1 is read-only). (5) Revert the test edit before Phase 2 ships.
verification_pattern.phase_2: Same as Phase 1, but step 4 changes: FUSE state monitor/prompts/tinker.md MUST now match git. Plus: monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json sentinel exists with copied_paths listing tinker.md.
verification_pattern.regression_canary: Tinker's pre-flight directive-discovery (in tinker.md, the part that grep'd PENDING DIRECTIVE candidates) reads from FUSE. If Phase 2 is working, tinker should NEVER again phantom-pickup a directive that's marked completed in git. The phantom-pickup FND from previous tinker runs is the canary; it should disappear from tinker reports after Phase 2.
verification_pattern.negative_canary: monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-<ts>.json sentinels should only appear during operator-initiated force-pushes. Spurious non-FF aborts indicate a bug in the merge-base check or a concurrent-push race.

blast_radius.phase_1: Single new bash block in monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md. Single new file pattern under monitor/integrity/. No build.js changes. No CLAUDE.md changes beyond a one-paragraph note. Zero FUSE writes. Trivially reversible.
blast_radius.phase_2: Same block extended with git merge + sync-workspace + sentinel writes. FUSE-side writes are governed by the existing build.js syncToWorkspace function (already-tested mechanism). Reversible by reverting the workspace-sync.md edit. No data migrations.
agents_affected: tinker (consumes new sentinel files for soft-complaints grep and phantom-pickup-elimination canary)
all FUSE-reading agents (benefit from synced FUSE state)
blast_radius.operator_workflow_changes: None for Phase 1. For Phase 2, operator-direct commits no longer need manual 'cp from clone to FUSE' or 'node build.js sync-workspace' invocation — the next hourly workspace-sync handles it. The operator's existing /tmp/edit-clone workflow is unchanged.

implementation_cost.phase_1: ~30 min Opus to edit workspace-sync.md + ~15 min operator review of the prompt diff before deployment.
implementation_cost.phase_2: After 7 days of Phase 1 logs: ~20 min Opus to extend the block + ~15 min operator review + ~10 min operator approval based on Phase 1 data.

operator_cost_estimate.current_cost_per_week: ~30 min/week per the directive's evidence section. Composition: tinker manual backfills (~5 min/week × 2-4 events), cowork sessions noticing divergence and re-cloning (~10 min/week), agents executing wasted runs on stale prompts (~10 min/week of unproductive Opus token spend). Plus the phantom-pickup-cost ~$2/Opus run × ~6 runs/day × 4-day gap-open phases ≈ ~$48 per recurrence, observed at least once in this 7-day window.
operator_cost_estimate.expected_savings_per_week_after_phase_2: ~25 min/week of human/Opus time + ~$10-50/week in wasted phantom-pickup Opus runs. Payback period for the ~75 min implementation cost: ~3 weeks.
operator_cost_estimate.ongoing_maintenance_cost: Near-zero. The mechanism uses the existing build.js OWNERSHIP table as its source-of-truth, so adding/removing git-owned files automatically updates the sync set. Sentinel files are auto-pruned by prune-integrity.js. The only ongoing operator attention is: review the per-cycle sync-workspace-runs sentinel if it shows unexpected file lists (rare) and respond to non-FF abort sentinels (rare, operator-driven anyway).

rejection_criteria: If operator decides discipline-only (option_b) is acceptable and is willing to enforce the dual-write rule via a structure-integrity gate that fails the build when a git-owned file diverges between FUSE and git. (Tinker recommends against this — see counter_considerations.)

If Phase 1 logs show <3 divergence events per week for 4 consecutive weeks, the implementation cost may not be worth the savings. In that case, the operator should manually cp + sync-workspace as a habit (return to option_b implicitly).
If the non-FF abort gate fires spuriously (>1 false positive per month), Phase 2 should be paused while the merge-base logic is re-examined.
non_goals: Does not modify workspace-sync's existing FUSE→git push behavior (PROP-051 safety gates A1-A4 preserved).
Does not extend workspace-sync's deletion authority. Phase 2 only ADDS to FUSE via copyFileSync; never deletes FUSE files.
Does not change build.js publish behavior. The existing 'publish' path continues to invoke syncToWorkspace at the end of its cycle as it always has.
Does not change the existing CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules. The new sentinel files fit under existing classifications.
Does not add per-agent pre-flight divergence checks (option_c). Reserved for Phase 3 if Phase 2 proves insufficient.
Does not modify the PROP-051 fail-closed safety gates (A1-A4).
related_props: PROP-010 (extracted sync-to-workspace logic — the mechanism this PROP invokes)
PROP-051 (workspace-sync safety gates A1-A4 — non-FF abort mirrors the A4 delete-gate pattern)
PROP-050 (Git Data API push fallback — one of the 'publish-bypassing' write paths that causes FUSE staleness)
PROP-045 (anti-reversion guard — same agent, smart_copy mtime+content-hash logic; conceptually related)
PROP-060 (tinker Mode 0 assigned-tinker scan — same defect class: writer field without consumer)
PROP-026 Phase 1 (decider BAU/burndown mode toggle — burndown lowers decider cadence which makes the FUSE-stale window longer; PROP-061 makes that less painful)
directive_reference: DIRECTIVE-20260526-001 (filed 2026-05-26T05:30Z low, bumped to HIGH 2026-05-26T20:30Z after observable staleness in multiple prompt files including tinker.md, decider.md, structure-integrity.md).
estimated_implementation_effort: Phase 1: ~30 min Opus + ~15 min operator review = ~45 min total. Phase 2: ~30 min Opus + ~25 min operator review + 7-day observation gap between phases. Total wall-clock from PROP approval to Phase 2 deployed: ~7-10 days, with ≤2 hours of human time.
implementation_notes: Phase 1 + Phase 2 applied together in one commit. The fast-forward-only gate provides the safety the Phase 1 observation week was meant to provide (non-FF aborts via sentinel; FF-only merge means no destructive operations; sync-workspace is idempotent with no delete logic). Bootstrap done manually before push to clear the existing FUSE staleness backlog (70 git-owned files copied, 12 new append-only files). Files modified: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (added Step 4c + Rules updates), monitor/scripts/prune-integrity.js (added 3 retention policies for new sentinel file types), CLAUDE.md (added FUSE-staleness mitigation paragraph).
